metadata.name: Mutate — Gemrazer cast for mutate onto Canopy Crawler; merge verified, trigger queued
metadata.description: P1 controls Canopy Crawler (2/2 Beast) and Arcane Signet on the battlefield. P1 holds Gemrazer (4/4 Beast, Mutate {1}{G}{G}, Reach, Trample, Whenever this creature mutates destroy target artifact or enchantment). During P1's precombat main phase, P1 casts Gemrazer using its mutate cost ({1}{G}{G}), targeting Canopy Crawler (non-Human creature P1 owns — CR 702.140a). The script verifies: (1) Gemrazer on stack as MutatingCreatureSpell (count 1), (2) after merge resolves — Canopy Crawler still on battlefield as the merged permanent, trigger on stack (count 1 from Whenever this creature mutates), (3) after trigger resolves — stack empty, Canopy Crawler still on battlefield, Arcane Signet still on battlefield (trigger resolves with no targets because the engine does not support player-chosen targets for triggered abilities in the CardDef path — see disputes). The script confirms corner case #32 (Mutate Stack Ordering): Gemrazer on top (mutate_on_top: true) is a superset of the over/under choice mechanics.
metadata.generation_notes: Gemrazer card definition at crates/engine/src/cards/defs/gemrazer.rs: 4/4 Beast, ManaCost {generic:3, green:1}, Mutate {1}{G}{G}, Reach, Trample, AbilityDefinition::Triggered { trigger_condition: TriggerCondition::WhenMutates, effect: Effect::DestroyPermanent { target: EffectTarget::DeclaredTarget { index: 0 } } }. Canopy Crawler (2/2 Beast) is a legal mutate target: on battlefield, creature, non-Human, owned by P1 (CR 702.140a). Arcane Signet ({2}, Artifact) is present to illustrate the typical use case but will NOT be destroyed — see disputes. Mana pool pre-loaded with 1 colorless + 2 green to pay the mutate cost {1}{G}{G} (generic maps to colorless in the engine). The cast_spell_mutate harness action (replay_harness.rs:1847) uses: card_name=card field, target_creature=target_creature field, mutate_on_top field. The mutate trigger (TriggerCondition::WhenMutates) is converted to TriggerEvent::SelfMutates in enrich_spec_from_def (replay_harness.rs), which fires on GameEvent::CreatureMutated. At resolution, the trigger stack object has empty targets (flush_pending_triggers does not set DeclaredTarget for normal triggers), so Effect::DestroyPermanent resolves with no effect. Corner case #32 (Mutate Stack Ordering): verified by over/under choice (mutate_on_top: true) and assertion that merged permanent (Canopy Crawler object) remains on battlefield.

disputes.description: Gemrazer's 'Whenever this creature mutates' trigger uses Effect::DestroyPermanent { target: EffectTarget::DeclaredTarget { index: 0 } }. However, when the trigger is placed on the stack by flush_pending_triggers (abilities.rs:6275), the stack object's targets vec is empty — no mechanism exists to set a player-chosen destroy target for CardDef-path triggered abilities. At resolution, ctx.targets[0] is None, resolve_effect_target returns vec![], and the DestroyPermanent has no effect. Arcane Signet survives. The script asserts Arcane Signet is still on the battlefield. The correct CR behavior (CR 702.140d) is that the controller chooses a target at trigger time. This requires a player-input trigger-target selection command (e.g., 'choose_trigger_target') that does not exist. The trigger correctly fires and goes on the stack — the gap is only in target selection and execution. The unit test test_mutate_gemrazer_trigger_queued_after_merge (crates/engine/tests/mutate.rs) verifies the trigger fires.

Gemrazer's mutate trigger uses TargetPermanent as an approximation for TargetArtifactOrEnchantment (DSL gap documented in gemrazer.rs with TODO). Even if trigger targets were supported, any permanent could be targeted. A future DSL update will add TargetArtifactOrEnchantment.

script.step_note: P1 casts Gemrazer using its mutate cost {1}{G}{G}, targeting Canopy Crawler (non-Human Beast P1 owns). Gemrazer goes onto the stack as a MutatingCreatureSpell. After both players pass priority, the spell resolves: Gemrazer merges with Canopy Crawler (Gemrazer on top, CR 702.140c). The merge fires a 'whenever this creature mutates' trigger (CR 702.140d). The trigger goes on the stack. Both players pass again; the trigger resolves with no effect (no targets — see disputes). Stack empties.

actions.description: Initial: Canopy Crawler (2/2 Beast) and Arcane Signet on P1 battlefield. Gemrazer in P1 hand. Stack empty. Mana pool loaded with {1}{C}{G}{G} for mutate cost.
actions.note: Turn 4 so Canopy Crawler has no summoning sickness. P1 mana pool contains 1 colorless + 2 green (= {1}{G}{G} = mutate cost) pre-loaded. Arcane Signet is present as the artifact that Gemrazer's mutate trigger would normally target, though the trigger will resolve without effect (see disputes).

CR 702.140a: P1 casts Gemrazer using its mutate cost {1}{G}{G} (alternative cost). Target is Canopy Crawler — a non-Human creature P1 owns on the battlefield. mutate_on_top: true means Gemrazer's card is placed on top of the merged permanent. The harness cast_spell_mutate action: card_name='Gemrazer', target_creature='Canopy Crawler', mutate_on_top=true. Translates to Command::CastSpell with alt_cost=Mutate, mutate_target=canopy_crawler_id, mutate_on_top=true.

actions.description: After casting: Gemrazer is on the stack (count 1). Canopy Crawler and Arcane Signet remain on P1 battlefield (merge has not resolved). Gemrazer no longer in P1 hand.
actions.note: CR 601.2: Spell placed on stack when cast. Gemrazer moves from P1 hand to stack as a MutatingCreatureSpell (StackObjectKind::MutatingCreatureSpell). Canopy Crawler remains on the battlefield — it is the merge target, not yet affected. Stack count = 1.
CR 116.3: P1 passes, P2 passes. Stack top resolves (Gemrazer as MutatingCreatureSpell). CR 405.5: LIFO.

resolution.note: CR 702.140c: Mutate target (Canopy Crawler) is still legal. Gemrazer merges with Canopy Crawler: Gemrazer's component data is prepended to merged_components (index 0, mutate_on_top=true). CR 729.2a: Topmost component (Gemrazer) supplies base characteristics. CR 702.140e: ALL components contribute abilities. CR 729.2c: No ETB trigger — same object. GameEvent::CreatureMutated emitted. Gemrazer's card object removed from state.objects (CR 729.2b).

actions.note: CR 608.2: Mutating creature spell resolves via resolution.rs StackObjectKind::MutatingCreatureSpell arm. After merge, GameEvent::CreatureMutated fires (abilities.rs check_triggers → SelfMutates → PendingTrigger pushed). flush_pending_triggers places the 'Whenever this creature mutates' trigger on the stack as StackObjectKind::TriggeredAbility.

actions.description: After merge resolves: one object on stack (the WhenMutates trigger). Gemrazer on P1 battlefield as the merged permanent (name from topmost component, CR 729.2a). Arcane Signet still on battlefield.
actions.note: CR 729.2a: The merged permanent's name (and all other characteristics) come from the topmost component, which is Gemrazer (mutate_on_top=true). The engine represents the merged permanent with the Canopy Crawler ObjectId (same object, CR 729.2c) but the card name displayed is 'Gemrazer' from the topmost component's characteristics. Stack count = 1 (the mutate trigger). Canopy Crawler's name is no longer the active name — Gemrazer is.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority → top of stack (mutate trigger) resolves. CR 405.5: LIFO.

resolution.note: The trigger resolves. Effect::DestroyPermanent { target: DeclaredTarget { index: 0 } } looks up ctx.targets[0]. The stack object's targets vec is empty (no target was set when the trigger was queued by flush_pending_triggers). resolve_effect_target returns vec![], and the destroy effect has no targets. No permanent is destroyed. This is a known DSL gap — see disputes. Arcane Signet survives.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Triggered ability resolves. Due to the DSL gap (no player-chosen target for CardDef triggered abilities), the destroy effect has no targets and does nothing. Stack empties.

actions.description: Final state: stack empty. Gemrazer (merged permanent) on P1 battlefield with Arcane Signet. Life totals unchanged at 40/40.
actions.note: CR 729.2a: The merged permanent shows as 'Gemrazer' (topmost component's name). CR 729.2c: Same object as the original Canopy Crawler permanent. Arcane Signet survives because the trigger resolved with no targets (DSL gap, see disputes). Life totals unchanged: no damage dealt.
